info.name: Help PR - All API routes
info.description: Postman Collection v2.1. Regenerate: node postman/generate-all-routes-collection.js. Import postman/Help-PR-All-Routes.postman_collection.json, then import postman/Local.postman_environment.json (or Staging) and select that environment. Collection Variables hold defaults; Environment overrides base_url, auth_token, and resource ids. Requests use {{base_url}}, {{auth_token}}, {{id}}.

variable.description: API root with protocol, no trailing slash (e.g. http://localhost:5001).
JWT / bearer token from /api/auth/userLogin or /api/auth/login. Prefer storing only in an Environment as type secret.
Generic MongoDB resource _id used in URLs like .../get/{{id}}, .../update/{{id}}.
State document _id (for request bodies linking to state).
Admin user _id (audit / created_by fields if applicable).
Free-text search for GET /api/quote/getAll (quote_sequence_id, quote_description, linked user/partner names, etc.).
GET /api/quote/getAll filter: new | pending | accepted | success | failed (same as getCounts / POST getCount quote-management). Alias fail → failed.

item.name: Category
item.description: Same request workflow as Service: types 5/6 direct publish; types 1/3 pending (is_active false). Super admin/staff approve or reject. getDropDown: catalog only.

request.description: Token type 5 or 6.
Types 1 or 3 → pending.
Filter pending queue.
Accepted / legacy only.
Same rules as create; bulk Excel requires types 5 or 6.

item.name: Service
item.description: Service workflow: Super Admin (5) / Staff (6) create published services directly (request_status accepted, is_request_mode false). Admin (1) / Employee (3) create pending requests (is_active forced false until approved). Super Admin / Staff can approve or reject with rejection_reason. Admin / Employee can edit only their own pending/request rows and cannot set request_status or rejection_reason. getDropDown returns only accepted catalog entries.

request.description: Use token for type 5 or 6. Creates accepted service immediately. Alternatively use multipart/form-data with file field `image` instead of image_url.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "New requested service",
  "desc": "Awaiting super admin approval",
  "price": 299,
  "category_id": "{{category_id}}",
  "city_ids": ["{{city_id}}"],
  "state_ids": ["{{state_id}}"],
  "image_url": "https://example.com/service.jpg",
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: Use token for type 1 or 3. Server sets request_status pending, is_request_mode true, is_active false until reviewed.
Only super admin or staff can filter by request_status.
Returns only accepted / legacy services (excludes pending requests).
Admin/employee: only on services they requested. Cannot send request_status or rejection_reason.
Without `records` array: same workflow as POST /create by role. Bulk Excel array import requires Super Admin or Staff.
Super Admin/Staff: any service. Admin/Employee: only their own request-mode rows.

item.name: Upload files (multipart)
item.description: Bearer token required. Body: form-data, key files (type File). Up to 5 files per route.

item.name: Update files (multipart)
item.description: Bearer token required. Body: form-data, key files (type File).

item.name: Quote
item.description:
Quote lifecycle: Create → Update (fields while **new**/**pending**, or **status** anytime allowed).

**`status` values (stored in DB):** `new`, `pending`, `accepted`, `success`, `failed`.

**Status via `PUT /api/quote/update/:id` only** — no separate approve/reject/cancel/convert routes.
- `{ "status": "accepted" }` — approve
- `{ "status": "failed", "rejection_reason": "..." }` — reject
- `{ "status": "failed", "cancellation_reason": "..." }` — cancel
- `{ "status": "success" }` — creates order (accepted quotes only); response includes `order` object

**Order creation:** `status: success` on update, or `POST /api/order/create` with `quote_id` when quote is **accepted**.

body.raw:
{
  "user_id": "{{user_id}}",
  "category_id": "{{category_id}}",
  "service_id": "{{service_id}}",
  "partner_id": "{{partner_id}}",
  "employee_id": "{{employee_id}}",
  "service_price": 1000,
  "franchise_id": "{{franchise_id}}",
  "address_id": "{{address_id}}",
  "created_by_id": "{{id}}",
  "from_date": "2026-05-08",
  "to_date": "2026-05-10",
  "work_hours_per_day": 8,
  "total_work_hours": 24,
  "work_start_time": "09:00",
  "work_end_time": "17:00",
  "quote_description": "Sample quote description for the requested work"
}

request.description: Creates a quote. Required: user_id, partner_id, category_id, service_id, franchise_id, address_id, service_price, from_date, to_date, work_hours_per_day, total_work_hours, work_start_time, work_end_time. Optional: employee_id, created_by_id, quote_description (max 1000 chars).
Returns one quote with populated relations. **`record.status`** is one of: new, pending, accepted, success, failed.

Paginated list. Each row **`status`**: new | pending | accepted | success | failed.

**Auth scope (from JWT, server-enforced):**
- **Super admin (5) / Staff (6):** all franchises; optional `franchise_id` query narrows.
- **Franchise admin (1) / Employee (3):** only quotes for their franchise (`user.franchise_id` or franchise where `admin_id` = caller). Other `franchise_id` query → **403**.
- **Partner / Customer:** **403** — use role-appropriate endpoints.

Also: page, limit, user_id, partner_id, employee_id, category_id, service_id, search, from_date, to_date, include_history, sort_by, sort_order.
Quotes for one customer. Each record **`status`**: new | pending | accepted | success | failed.

Partial update. Send at least one allowed field.

**Fields (new/pending only):** partner_id, employee_id, category_id, service_id, franchise_id, address_id, service_price, from_date, to_date, work_hours_per_day, total_work_hours, work_start_time, work_end_time, created_by_id, quote_description.

**Status (any allowed transition):** `status` — new | pending | accepted | success | failed. Optional `rejection_reason` / `cancellation_reason` when setting **failed**.

**`status: success`** creates an order (quote must be **accepted**). Response includes `order: { order_id, unique_id }`.

**quote_description:** customer, assigned employee, franchise admin, super admin, or staff only.
Pending/new → **accepted**. Does not create an order.
Sets **failed** with optional rejection_reason.
Sets **failed** with optional cancellation_reason.
**Accepted** quote only. Creates order (same as POST /api/order/create), sets quote **order_id** and **status** **success**. Response includes `order.order_id`, `order.unique_id`.

Dashboard card counts — same buckets as **GET /api/quote/getAll?status=** and **POST /api/getCount** `{ "type": "quote-management" }`.

**Response `record` keys (all numbers):**
| Key | Meaning |
|-----|--------|
| `new` | Pending, no partner assigned |
| `pending` | Pending, partner assigned |
| `accepted` | Approved / converted, no order yet |
| `success` | Converted with `order_id` set |
| `failed` | Rejected, cancelled, expired, or approved without order |

Uses `buildQuoteBucketFilter` (string status in DB and legacy numeric 1–6). Optional query: **franchise_id** (caller must have access). List/detail rows use the same bucket names in **`status`** (no separate status_code in latest API).
Soft-deletes the quote (deleted_at).
